title: What is Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD)?
text:
Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD)
Driven by the European Union’s 2026 chemical and industrial strategy mandates, the Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD) framework establishes an upstream, proactive paradigm that integrates safety, circularity, and functional performance from the earliest stages of material and process engineering (Liao et al. 2026).
Rather than relying on retrospective, “end-of-pipe” pollution abatement strategies, SSbD shifts the compliance burden into early-stage product lifecycle design. This model systematically preempts industrial risk by evaluating material flows, environmental toxicities, and occupational health hazards across the complete value chain (Ang and Liao forthcoming, 2026).

title: What is Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD)?
text:
References


Ang, Karen, and Han-Teng Liao. forthcoming, 2026. “Scoping Review of AI, Metrology, and ESG in the Semiconductor Sector: Implications for Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD).” 2026 IEEE International Conference on Engineering, Technology, and Innovation (ICE/ITMC), forthcoming, 2026. https://doi.org/10.31235/osf.io/yknwt_v1.


Liao, Han-Teng, and Karen Ang. forthcoming, 2026. “From Stacks to Circuits: A Regenerative Socio-Technical Roadmap for AI Infrastructure Within Planetary Boundaries.” 2026 IEEE International Conference on Engineering, Technology, and Innovation (ICE/ITMC), forthcoming, 2026. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2606.10544.


Liao, Han-Teng, Chang-Yi Kao, and Karen Ang. 2026. Trustworthy Smart Fabs via Professional Proxies: Scaling Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD) Through Industrial Data Spaces. arXiv:2606.09227. arXiv. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2606.09227.

title: Han-Teng Liao: CV & Blog
Technology Roadmapping for the Twin Transition in Global Business Services
text:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
Penang, Malaysia – June 2, 2026 – The Safe-and-Sustainable-by-Design (SSbD) Chips Value Chain Services (SCVCS) initiative is pleased to announce that its research, titled “Orchestrating the Twin Transition in Multinational Corporations: Technology Roadmapping for Green and Digital Global Business Services,” has been formally accepted for presentation at the 32nd IEEE ICE/ITMC Conference in Porto, Portugal.

Research Contribution: Bridging Green and Digital Strategies
This research addresses the challenge of synchronizing digital transformation with sustainability objectives within large-scale operations. Led by the SCVCS team, the study synthesizes Technology Roadmapping (TRM) and the ITU innovation toolkit to identify Global Business Services (GBS) units as “operational airlocks.” These units play a vital role in mediating between broader environmental goals and concrete digital execution, helping to manage the structural complexity of INN 2 (inclusive and accessible innovation practices) and INN 3 (global response to EU-level coordination and standardization).
Furthermore, our work highlights that after foundational roadmapping, INN 4 requires a human-centric shift to ensure that GBS units can effectively orchestrate and govern AI-native transitions. Finally, by framing GBS as strategic “operational airlocks,” we address INN 5 by defining GBS as a multinational corporation’s living laboratory. As multinational corporations (MNCs) face increasing pressure to harmonize digital efficiency with environmental stewardship, our research derives a socio-technical framework that treats GBS units as the primary nexus for these complex transitions.


Invitation for Collaboration
The SCVCS initiative welcomes engagement with industry leaders, academic researchers, and sustainability practitioners to further develop these roadmapping frameworks for cross-sectoral interoperability and improved organizational alignment during the Twin Transition.
The SCVCS initiative remains dedicated to advancing the 2026 European Commission SSbD Stakeholder Agenda and the IEEE IRDS roadmaps, ensuring that regulatory compliance serves as a catalyst for industrial innovation in Industry 5.0 ecosystems.

title: Trustworthy Smart Fabs via Professional Proxies
text:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
Penang, Malaysia – May 27, 2026 – The Safe-and-Sustainable-by-Design (SSbD) Chips Value Chain Services (SCVCS) initiative is pleased to announce that its latest research, titled “Trustworthy Smart Fabs via Professional Proxies: Scaling Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD) through Industrial Data Spaces,” has been formally accepted for presentation in the proceedings of the 32nd IEEE ICE/ITMC Conference.

Research Contribution: Bridging Compliance and Innovation
The research introduces the “Professional Proxies” framework—a novel approach to managing regulatory governance within semiconductor manufacturing. Authored by Han-Teng Liao (Independent Researcher), Chang-Yi Kao (Soochow University), and Karen Ang (Independent Researcher), the study addresses the critical “Data Sovereignty Paradox” faced by advanced “Smart Fabs”. By implementing autonomous agentic workflows within hardware-isolated Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs), the framework enables fabs to verify sustainability profiles without exposing proprietary manufacturing recipes.


Acknowledgment of Review Resources
The authors extend their sincere gratitude to the organizers and the Review Committee of Special Session [SS04]: Trustworthy Autonomous AI for Digital Transformation, chaired by Dr. Nahid Farhady Ghalaty, Jordan Hull, and Abhilasha Bhargav-Spantzel (Microsoft). While the team will not be presenting on-site in Porto, the SCVCS initiative deeply values the helpful reviews and critiques provided by this committee. The insights gained from this peer-review process are currently being incorporated into an expanded version of the manuscript, which the team intends to submit for formal journal publication, where the conference and committee’s rigorous contributions will be fully acknowledged.


Invitation for Collaboration
The SCVCS initiative welcomes engagement with industry leaders, academic researchers, and sustainability practitioners to further develop these frameworks for cross-sectoral interoperability and molecule-to-market traceability.
The SCVCS initiative remains dedicated to advancing the 2026 European Commission SSbD Stakeholder Agenda and the IEEE IRDS roadmaps, ensuring that regulatory compliance serves as a catalyst for industrial innovation in Industry 5.0 ecosystems.

title: Semiconductor Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD)
text:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
Penang, Malaysia – June 1, 2026 – The Safe-and-Sustainable-by-Design (SSbD) Chips Value Chain Services (SCVCS) initiative is pleased to announce that its research, titled “Scoping Review of AI, Metrology, and ESG in the Semiconductor Sector: Implications for Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD),” has been formally accepted for presentation at the 32nd IEEE ICE/ITMC Conference in Porto, Portugal.

Research Contribution: Architecting SSbD Systems
This study addresses the complex integration required to achieve sustainable semiconductor manufacturing. Led by the SCVCS team, the research proposes a novel 6-layer System of Systems (SoS) architecture. This framework is designed to integrate AI-driven metrology, supply chain Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) metrics, and federated industrial data spaces into a unified platform for SSbD manufacturing execution.
The SCVCS initiative deeply values the rigorous peer-review provided by this committee. Our contribution aligns with these themes by operationalizing a multi-layered SoS architecture that bridges TEC 1 (AI-supported roadmapping) and TEC 2 (Digital Transformation for Competitiveness) through TEC 3 (Federated and secure industrial data spaces). Furthermore, our work highlights that after foundational systems thinking across metrology and AI, TEC 4 requires attention to compliance and transparency. Finally, by embedding SSbD as the core operational logic, we address TEC 5 by establishing climate-resilient, low-carbon manufacturing trajectories that translate atomic-scale process precision into verifiable environmental sustainability.


Invitation for Collaboration
The SCVCS initiative welcomes engagement with industry leaders, academic researchers, and sustainability practitioners to further develop these architectural frameworks for cross-sectoral interoperability and enhanced manufacturing sustainability.
The SCVCS initiative remains dedicated to advancing the 2026 European Commission SSbD Stakeholder Agenda and the IEEE IRDS roadmaps, ensuring that regulatory compliance serves as a catalyst for industrial innovation in Industry 5.0 ecosystems.

title: From ‘Electrons-to-Tokens’ Stacks to ‘Atoms-to-Values’ Circuits
text:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
Penang, Malaysia – June 4, 2026 – The Safe-and-Sustainable-by-Design (SSbD) Chips Value Chain Services (SCVCS) initiative is proud to present its research mapping the physical boundaries of digital scale, titled “From Stacks to Circuits: A Regenerative Socio-Technical Roadmap for AI Infrastructure within Planetary Boundaries,” accepted for publication at the 32nd IEEE ICE/ITMC Conference.

Research Contribution: Reframing Infrastructure Paths
This study introduces a Regenerative Socio-Technical roadmap that reframes AI infrastructure as a system-of-systems governed ultimately by planetary limits. By integrating the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers International Roadmap for Devices and Systems (IEEE IRDS) sustainability considerations for semiconductor facilities, the study proposes a metabolic circuit framework that centers “Values and Needs” within production and consumption loops.
The SCVCS initiative deeply values the rigorous peer-review provided by the committee, including Prof. Ricardo Jardim Goncalves (New University of Lisbon) and José Ferreira (UNINOVA). This work provides a verifiable mechanism for resource efficiency and product lifecycle transparency that responds directly to real-world infrastructure constraints—such as the recent grid stress limits observed in Taiwan. By anchoring artificial intelligence within a metabolic circuit, the study demonstrates how a spontaneous order of resource parsimony can foster industrial resilience in the digital circular economy.


Invitation for Collaboration
The SCVCS Initiative welcomes engagement with industry leaders, academic researchers, and sustainability practitioners to further develop these frameworks for cross-sectoral interoperability and molecule-to-market traceability.
The SCVCS initiative remains dedicated to advancing the 2026 European Commission SSbD Stakeholder Agenda and the 2024 IRDS roadmaps, ensuring that regulatory compliance serves as a catalyst for industrial innovation in Industry 5.0 ecosystems.

title: What is IEEE IRDS?
text:
IEEE IRDS
By expert representatives from global semiconductor manufacturers and related experts, the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) International Roadmap for Devices and Systems (IRDS) establishes a comprehensive strategic framework for advanced hardware acceleration, microelectronics, and computing systems.
It represents a collective multi-stakeholder consensus engineered by expert representatives from global semiconductor manufacturers, original equipment manufacturers (OEMs), materials suppliers, and analytical research laboratories.

title: What is IEEE IRDS?
text:
Many Roadmaps?
Ultimately, the roadmaps operate as the definitive technological baselines for many aspects of semidconductor manufacturing. They are thus useful for evaluating modern fabrication capabilities(Ang and Liao forthcoming, 2026) and guiding scaling trajectories within global value chains(Liao and Ang forthcoming, 2026).

title: What is IEEE IRDS?
text:
EEE IRDS ESHS
Focusing on sustainability on and around the semiconductor facilities, the Environment, Safety, Health & Sustainability (ESHS) chapter of the roadmap defines quantitative benchmarks and actionable targets for building climate-resilient fabrication facilities[Liao and Ang (forthcoming, 2026)](Ang and Liao forthcoming, 2026).
Grounded in facility-monitoring, it outlines critical tracking metrics including freshwater usage, evaporative water losses, hazardous waste generation, and energy consumption factors. However, contemporary reviews identify ongoing technology gaps within the roadmap, particularly regarding real-time chemical intensity tracking, Scope 1 greenhouse gas tool abatement, and Scope 3 supply-chain footprint blind spots.

title: What is IEEE IRDS?
text:
ESHS as Sites of Practice
Semiconductor facilities become important sites of practice and research for smart sustainable manufacturing to integrate best Industry 5.0 with advanced RegTech and FinTech (Ang and Liao forthcoming, 2026; Liao et al. 2026).

Maps directly to the Twin Green & Digital Transition.
Bridges factory-floor operational data with macro ESG frameworks.

title: What is IEEE IRDS?
text:
See Also: Semi Standards - 1
The IRDS framework explicitly cross-references established SEMI standards to govern manufacturing sustainability metrics, particularly leveraging the SEMI S Series (Safety, Environmental & Energy) to calculate Equivalent Energy Consumption Factors (Liao and Ang forthcoming, 2026).

title: What is IEEE IRDS?
text:
See Also: Semi Standards - 2
This coordination establishes unified financial and environmental benchmarking metrics, allowing technology managers to balance tool-level operational constraints with localized utility realities.
Other relevant SEMI standards consists of SEMI MF Series (Metrology), SEMI T Series (Traceability) and SEMI F Series (Facilities). Also SEMI MS Series and SEMI C Series can have specific material flow requirements for the SEMI M Series (Wafers & Process Control) to implement a System of Systems (SoS) orchestration.

title: What is IEEE IRDS?
text:
References


Ang, Karen, and Han-Teng Liao. forthcoming, 2026. “Scoping Review of AI, Metrology, and ESG in the Semiconductor Sector: Implications for Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD).” 2026 IEEE International Conference on Engineering, Technology, and Innovation (ICE/ITMC), forthcoming, 2026. https://doi.org/10.31235/osf.io/yknwt_v1.
